operating_state: NIKE revenues increased 1% to $11.7 billion driven by North America growth of 4% and EMEA expansion of 6%, offset by Greater China decline of 9% and Converse contraction of 27%. NIKE Brand wholesale revenues rose 7% while NIKE Direct revenues fell 4% due to 12% decline in digital sales. Footwear revenues decreased 1% despite 2% unit growth as average selling prices dropped 4 percentage points from higher discounts and channel mix shifts.
cost_structure: Gross margin contracted 320 basis points to 42.2% primarily from lower average selling prices reducing margin by 250 basis points and higher product costs including new tariffs reducing margin by 100 basis points. Total selling and administrative expenses decreased 1% to 34.3% of revenues as demand creation expense fell 3% and operating overhead remained flat despite wage inflation.
material_events: Company returned $0.7 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases during the quarter. Management filed a shelf registration statement permitting unlimited securities issuance through July 2028. Shareholders approved amended stock incentive plan increasing authorized shares by 45 million at September annual meeting. Company corrected prior disclosure error regarding product purchase obligations of $5 billion.
macro_exposures: New tariffs created gross incremental cost of approximately $1.5 billion on annualized basis with negative impact expected on fiscal 2026 gross margin. Company faces ongoing uncertainty from geopolitical dynamics, tax regulation changes, fluctuating foreign currency exchange rates and evolving tariff policies that could materially impact consumer behavior and profitability.
forward_outlook: Management is reducing supply of certain footwear products while shifting to new innovative products and rebalancing portfolio mix. Company is repositioning NIKE Brand Digital as full-price platform and reinvesting in wholesale distribution while liquidating inventory through increased markdowns. Management is increasing investment in demand creation including brand marketing and sports marketing to support key product launches.
uncertainty_profile: Company continues navigating external factors creating operating environment volatility including import and export policy changes that could require business model modifications. Actions to rebalance product portfolio and marketplace positioning will have negative near-term impact on revenues and profitability with timing varying by segment. Management believes these strategic actions will reignite brand momentum for long-term shareholder value creation.
